title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 -  11 - 2017: Google Earthing Roman Coloseum's ropes

enrichment.summary: Kyle films an early-morning vlog from Boulder, giving a progress update on his Google Earth project recreating ancient Rome, with the Roman Colosseum (including its rope/awning system) as the current focus. He also briefly mentions listening to Gary Staddle on political systems, the audiobook of 'Fire and Fury', and Trump's 'Art of the Deal'.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: His favorite building — a 2000-year-old structure he finds remarkable for its modernity and uniqueness
Wishes he had used SketchUp instead but accepts his tracing approach as sufficient for the visual goal; also notes it costs $700 he doesn't have
Finds it entertaining as a portrait of Trump conning people, thinks Trump sounds smart but dishonest
Enjoyed it, found Trump's FBI speech quoted in it hilarious; thinks Trump is crazy

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got to sleep like 6 in the morning and then I was lying there and I thought to myself, I've got to do a video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The last time I did a video, I was just getting started with the ropes of the Coliseum.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today's the second full day working on the Coliseum project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm on rope 72 or something, maybe 160, in my project of the Google Earth thing at Brown.

claims.subject: the Coliseum
claims.claim: The ropes around the Coliseum are going to take the title of the most old and jeep thing.

claims.subject: the Coliseum
claims.claim: When the ropes are there and the awning is on it, you'll see instantaneously how it works.

claims.subject: the Coliseum
claims.claim: It'll look beautiful because it's a big giant tip.

claims.claim: I don't think I'll be done with the Coliseum by Sunday because I have to draw the bleachers.

claims.claim: There are 22 bleachers just on the second row section.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't mind spending so much time on the Google Earth thing to call it soon because I've always been fascinated with that building itself.

claims.subject: the Coliseum
claims.claim: The Coliseum has always been my favorite building because it's a 2000-year-old modern building.

claims.subject: the Coliseum
claims.claim: 2000 years ago, it was amazing for some barbarian use to just run through the woods naked.

claims.subject: the Coliseum
claims.claim: The Coliseum is the only one that still exists and is intact in Rome.

claims.subject: SketchUp
claims.claim: I read about a program called SketchUp where you could build models for Google Earth.

claims.subject: SketchUp
claims.claim: SketchUp is probably the stuff you see when you click 3D on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I would have bought SketchUp and done the project with that program instead of what I'm doing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think Rollets is probably better because it's just tracing, which is all I really wanted in the first place.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I just wanted something that you could look at and know what it is and how it looks.

claims.subject: SketchUp
claims.claim: With SketchUp, you can travel in the buildings and see through them from up above.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It'd be easy to do it to where I left stuff, and I think it's worth it to build it in Google Earth.

claims.subject: SketchUp
claims.claim: SketchUp costs 700 bucks, and I don't have 700 dollars.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I would have probably done it in the third program if I had thought more instead of just diving in.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: After I'm done with the Coliseum, I'm going to build the Circus Maximus, the Leicand Theater, and the other amphitheaters.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It'll be fresh in my mind, and I'll build it really quickly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll be on my way to finishing Rome, and it's going to be too lazy to show them straight.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to play the green screen in the background, but trust me, it'll look really good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll be done in a couple of months, rebuilding the whole city to my liking.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to Gary Staddle's politics over and over again.

claims.subject: Gary Staddle
claims.claim: Gary Staddle talks about all the different social-political systems in the novel Ocean between them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can never remember what Gary Staddle said, but I'll talk about that one.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: I was listening to Donald Trump's speech to the FBI, which was hilarious.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: I tried to find Donald Trump's speech to the FBI again on Google, but I only found the one where he spoke to the cadets of the Academy.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: I'm enjoying the deal, which is 11 hours long, and the Art of the Deal is amazing.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: The Art of the Deal is just Donald Trump trying to skin-scan people over and over again.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Donald Trump sounds smart and honest in the Art of the Deal.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I've talked enough, and I'm going to get a slate.
